definition: The dollar is in a late-stage transitional regime characterized by DXY lower highs (114.8→108.0→107.3) over 42 months and narrowing US growth differential (+2.0pp to +0.5-1.0pp), but without a confirmed weakening cycle. No catalyst equivalent to Plaza Accord (1985) or current account crisis (2002) is identifiable. The extended 42-month transition suggests genuinely opposing forces producing an unstable equilibrium.
evidence: DXY has not broken below 100 support
Current account deficit (~3-4% GDP) below 2006 peak (~6%)
AI capex inflows and fiscal smirk vs. BEER overvaluation, twin deficits, and growth convergence
pair_2 called this the most robust shared conclusion
open_questions: Will resolution be toward further weakening or renewed strengthening?
What is the catalyst threshold for breaking below DXY 100?
Does the 2018-19 muddled middle (22 months range-trading) repeat?
Is the fiscal dollar smirk durable support or time bomb?
